title: July 14th, 2022

 lyrics

[every](every) line a hook, look
i ain't writing a book
took my time and i put
my mind in a box
so you could try an open the top
climb in and drop
hopin the next stop is the kind that you want
only to find it's much bigger inside than it ought
the figures don't add up
in my mind it's an opp but you can't call for back-up
no one knows when it's time to watch
asleep on the stakeout so we act up
colored jokes to cover the fact that we crack up
yokes on us
gettin egged on by bacon
battered and stacked up
break fast or wait that's just the start of the day job
you got a lotta shit to eat on the way up
gotta beef up you wanna play ball
the plan is to show up and stand tall
got nothing to show for it, that's a bad call
fire the coach, the ref, and the front office
star get the bag and then bag y'all
cuz you've been an asshole
ownership's like that
on this ship i cap'n
let you line up when i make my mind up
i don't need thanks for the job
just pick, you want the plank or the swab
see we sea bound so those the only two ways to get off
can't get off course cuz the course is the way i'm weighing my choices
can't hand out free [food](food) cuz i'm paying in portions
stingy with with the see through so what i say is important
you tired, well me too but right now is for working
do what i tell you and this might serve long enough be worth it


i need more relationships where someone actively likes me but our attachment isn't dramatic. i think that's something friendship delivers. but that's me editing cuz i really just don't know if i have any active friendships. and when my other relationships have interconnecting friendships i really notice the difference. my inclusion in friend groups is very weak. and it's not something i [ask](ask) for or want. i also feel like my dramatic relationships overwhelm my social resources and i don't have much left for exploring. these things all feed into each other too. as my resources fail, other relationships build up so it's hard to relax and recover when alone time is the right medicine. and i'm reminded of it contemporarily, not just in my rememberings
